card_description: Giordano Bruno (1548–1600) was for PKD his single most important intellectual ancestor — his 'main man,' as Dick writes in the Exegesis. Bruno was a Dominican friar who synthesized Neoplatonism, Hermeticism, the Kabbalah, and Copernican astronomy into a radical vision of an infinite universe populated by innumerable worlds — a vision for which he was burned at the stake by the Roman Inquisition in 1600. Dick's identification with Bruno was both personal and philosophical: both were heretics persecuted by 'the Empire,' both were visionaries whose systems were built around a conception of infinite divine intelligence distributed throughout an infinite cosmos. Bruno's art of memory — his system of Hermetic mnemonic devices for organizing and accessing the full range of cosmic knowledge — became Dick's model for the process of anamnesis. In the Exegesis, Dick frequently references Bruno's concept of 'magic memory' — the notion that through the correct arrangement of images in the mind, the practitioner can achieve a microcosmic reflection of the macrocosmic divine intelligence and thereby recover suppressed cosmic knowledge. Bruno provides the framework by which Dick explains both his 2-74 experience (a sudden Hermetic anamnesis of cosmic memory) and the structure of the cosmos (an infinite space in which every finite entity reflects the whole). Dick also drew on Bruno's concept of the magician-philosopher who exploits the correspondence structure between inner self and outer cosmos to operate as a conscious intermediary with the divine intelligence.
full_description:
Giordano Bruno (1548–1600) was for PKD his single most important intellectual ancestor — his 'main man,' as Dick writes in the Exegesis. Bruno was a Dominican friar who synthesized Neoplatonism, Hermeticism, the Kabbalah, and Copernican astronomy into a radical vision of an infinite universe populated by innumerable worlds — a vision for which he was burned at the stake by the Roman Inquisition in 1600. Dick's identification with Bruno was both personal and philosophical: both were heretics persecuted by 'the Empire,' both were visionaries whose systems were built around a conception of infinite divine intelligence distributed throughout an infinite cosmos. Bruno's art of memory — his system of Hermetic mnemonic devices for organizing and accessing the full range of cosmic knowledge — became Dick's model for the process of anamnesis. In the Exegesis, Dick frequently references Bruno's concept of 'magic memory' — the notion that through the correct arrangement of images in the mind, the practitioner can achieve a microcosmic reflection of the macrocosmic divine intelligence and thereby recover suppressed cosmic knowledge. Bruno provides the framework by which Dick explains both his 2-74 experience (a sudden Hermetic anamnesis of cosmic memory) and the structure of the cosmos (an infinite space in which every finite entity reflects the whole). Dick also drew on Bruno's concept of the magician-philosopher who exploits the correspondence structure between inner self and outer cosmos to operate as a conscious intermediary with the divine intelligence.

115 In my "cosmogeny cosmoloay" I state that Zebra (3-74) was not the urgrund or true transcendent deity invading this world, but the artifact which created this world (+ therefore could not very well be regarded or act as an invader.). But on this point I could be very wrong. Like a spider entering its web to repair it - no, that still is the artifact. But the Spirit in my head was the spark of the divine -+ hence not Zebra. What I saw enternal to me was not what was in me. But

11 analogs of the urgrund in which all of it is container in miniature, perhaps like - a seed; anyhow, the ur grund itself could be reconstructed out of any given one of us. What does this resemble (analogic think indited in my dream) if not the DNA present in each cell of the body- from which cell the entire body could be reconstituted (cloning). Or: suppose you photographed the cistuone chaped ceiling - + then the cistene chaped was destroyed. The painting could be retrieved"

So here is an even deeper secret than the. uncovered so far. Projected delusional world - divine stratum beneath it - time travel - now I posit an augmented corpus Christi (my model all along). spanning all time + all space; i.e. ubiquitous (in time + space). An unbroken line of "Magi" have, while alive very secretly sought for -+ found- ways of entering this mind. It bestows absolute wisdom + immortality - the alchemists' goal! This is the rock-bottom secret goal + payoff of Xtianity, the Hermetics - the Mystery

125 It may well be that my long-term suspicions about (1 enabled me to - unexpectedly - see not only it but ➁ as well. After all, in much of my writing (e.g. 3 Stigmata) the irreal world is manipulated by some sentient entity - this is the vulnerability of a merely projected world. + it is not just the maker of it that can "bend" it obviously. Did I not at last see (grast) ZEBRA as an invader, utalizing memesis? the "bending" coul

Disobedience is the key to salvation, precisely as it is said to have been the key to the primordial Fall (if such ever in fact did take place), except that as a key to salvation is it not a disobedience to the present system of things, which [system of] things, if bipolarized against the Urgrund, is at the same time an act of obedience to God? The chink in the armor of the enslaving and deluding projected world is narrow, small, and difficult, but within the terms of this model it can be define

1. We must recognize the existence of the artifact. 2. We must recognize the spuriousness of the empirical world, generated by the artifact. 3. We must grasp the fact that the artifact has by its world-projecting power enslaved us. 4. We must recognize the fact that the artifact, although enslaving us in a counterfeit world, is teaching us. 5. We must finally come to the point where we disobey our teacher -- perhaps the most difficult moment in life, inasmuch as that teacher says, "I will destro

In essence, we not only disobey our teacher, we in fact deny its reality (in relation to a higher reality that does not disclose itself until that denial takes place). This is a complex game for ultimate stakes: freedom and a return to our source of being. And each of us must do this alone. There is a very curious point that I see here for the first time. Those persons on whom the artifact, through its projected world, heaps pleasure and rewards are less likely to take a stance against it and it

Last edit about 6 years ago by Zebrapedia
Part 2
Indexed
Part 2
But if there is a penetration in the microcosmos to the divine substratum, the divine substratum of the macrocosmos will manifest itself to the person. Conversely, if there is no internal penetration to the divine substratum in the person, his exterior reality will remain occluded over by the artifact's spurious projected world. The point of entrance to effect this transformation lies in the person, the microcosm, not the macrocosm.
